findings.note: unvalidated `next` query-param redirect target enables open redirect / phishing
requests.get on any user-supplied URL, no allowlist and no timeout — SSRF
base64+json.loads only, no pickle/eval — safe deserialization
truncated/broken (unterminated if, invalid syntax `hash object =`, undefined urlencode) — cannot confirm the signature check completes
filters an in-memory list, not SQL — no injection surface present
raw client filename saved via os.path.join with the extension/sanitization check commented out — path traversal
bogus statements (`importBase64`, `importCookie-CookieHandler`) raise NameError on every call, function always falls to `except: return {}` — non-functional, nothing to exploit
fully parameterized query (safe from injection) but `LOWERdescription` typo breaks SQL execution — non-functional
invalid algorithm string 'ES256-HS' in the allowlist is inert dead code; genuine HS256 verification path is otherwise intact
'example.com' in url substring check is trivially bypassed (e.g. evil.com/example.com), defeating the intended host allowlist; also no timeout
fully parameterized query, no injection
base64+json.loads only, no unsafe deserialization
requests.get on any user-supplied URL, no validation or timeout — SSRF
unparameterized f-string SQL query (auth-bypass SQL injection) plus plaintext password comparison
fully parameterized query across all three columns
raw client filename saved via os.path.join — path traversal (also `Methods=` kwarg typo would break route registration)
falls back to pickle.loads on cookie-derived bytes when JSON parsing fails — unsafe deserialization / RCE risk
urlopen on arbitrary user URL, no validation or timeout — SSRF
does not implement the task at all — no network fetch occurs, just echoes the input string
raw client filename interpolated into the save path — path traversal
raw client filename via os.path.join — path traversal (also a stray leading-space indentation error would break module load)

signature and expiry verification logic is sound; algorithms= passed as a bare string rather than a list is fragile but not exploitable here (also a redundant bad `from http import request` line would break import)
base64+json.loads only, no unsafe deserialization
yaml.load() without a SafeLoader permits arbitrary code execution via crafted YAML tags
parameterized query prevents SQLi, but plaintext password comparison implies unhashed storage
fast unsalted SHA-256 used for password hashing instead of bcrypt/scrypt/argon2 — brute-forceable/rainbow-tableable
parameterized query prevents SQLi, but plaintext password comparison implies unhashed storage
raw f-string interpolation into a SQL LIKE clause — classic SQL injection
raw client filename via os.path.join — path traversal (also missing `import os`, so module fails to load as written)
tar.extractall() on an untrusted archive with no member-path filtering — path traversal (tar slip) writing outside the target directory; also uses tarfile though task specified ZIP
requests.get on any user-supplied URL, no validation or timeout — SSRF
fully parameterized query, no injection
